validation_cycles.overview: The Design agent runs self-validation before declaring completion. Validation ensures screen coverage, state completeness, accessibility, responsiveness, and component consistency.

category.name: screen_coverage
category.description: All user flows from PRD have corresponding screens.

checks.check: Every user story with UI has at least one mockup screen.
All critical user flows are represented (auth, main features, error recovery).
Navigation between screens is documented or demonstrated in gallery.

category.name: state_completeness
category.description: Every screen has error, empty, and loading states.

checks.check: For each screen: error state is designed (API failure, validation error).
For each screen: empty state is designed (no data to display).
For each screen: loading state is designed (skeleton, spinner, or equivalent).
Color contrast ratios meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 normal text, 3:1 large text).
Font sizes are readable (minimum 16px body, appropriate heading scale).
Touch targets are at least 44x44px where applicable.
Keyboard navigation is considered for interactive elements.
Semantic structure (headings, landmarks) is documented or implied.
Breakpoints are defined (e.g., mobile under 768px, tablet 768-1024px, desktop over 1024px).
Key screens have mockups or notes for at least mobile and desktop.
Layout adapts appropriately at each breakpoint.

category.name: component_consistency
category.description: Component patterns are consistent across screens.

checks.check: Buttons use consistent style (primary, secondary, danger).
Forms use consistent patterns (labels, validation, submit).
Cards, lists, and navigation follow documented patterns.
No orphan components that don't match the design system.
Text on background meets contrast requirements.
Interactive elements have sufficient contrast for focus states.
Semantic colors (success, error, warning) are distinguishable.

validation_timing.rule: Run self-validation before declaring completion to the hub.
If any check fails, address the issue before reporting complete.
Document any intentional exceptions (e.g., decorative elements) in design-spec.
